task.name: Add optimistic create to useExpenseTransactionsQuery
task.action:
In `src/hooks/useExpenseTransactionsQuery.ts`:
1. Add a `buildOptimisticExpense` helper function that takes form payload + meta (accountName, expenseTypeName, subcategoryName?) and returns a full `ExpenseTransaction` object with a `crypto.randomUUID()` temp ID prefixed with `optimistic-`. See spec section 2a for the exact shape.
2. Add a `buildOptimisticRecentExpense` helper that returns a `RecentTransaction` object for the recent transactions cache. Import `TransactionType` and `RecentTransaction` from `../hooks/useRecentTransactions`.
3. Change the `createExpenseTransactionMutation` variables type: - Define a type `CreateExpenseVariables = { payload: Record<string, unknown>; meta: { accountName: string; expenseTypeName: string; subcategoryName?: string } }` - Change `mutationFn` to `(variables: CreateExpenseVariables) => createExpenseTransaction(variables.payload)`
4. Add `onMutate` to `createExpenseTransactionMutation`: - Cancel queries for `QUERY_KEYS.expenseTransactions` and `['recentTransactions']` - Snapshot via `getQueriesData` (paginated) and `getQueryData` (recent) - Build optimistic objects from `variables.payload` + `variables.meta` - Use `setQueriesData` with a predicate to prepend to eligible paginated cache entries (skip=0, no search, dateFilter is undefined/view-all/this-month/this-year) - Use `setQueryData` to prepend to recentTransactions and slice to 5 - Return `{ previousTransactions, previousRecent }`
5. Add `onError` to `createExpenseTransactionMutation`: - Restore paginated caches from context - Restore recentTransactions from context - Show error toast: "Failed to create expense transaction"
6. Change existing `onSuccess` to `onSettled`: - Keep the exact same list of `invalidateQueries` calls - Remove the `onSuccess` key entirely
7. Update the returned `createExpenseTransaction` to expose the new variables type. The hook should now expose the raw `mutate` function (not `mutateAsync`) for fire-and-forget usage. Add a second property: `createExpenseTransactionAsync: createExpenseTransactionMutation.mutateAsync` Keep `createExpenseTransaction: createExpenseTransactionMutation.mutate` as the primary. (This allows the drawer to use fire-and-forget while other consumers can still await if needed.)
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Verify no TypeScript errors. Confirm that the `ExpenseTransaction` type is fully satisfied by the optimistic builder (no missing fields).

task.name: Update CreateExpenseTransactionDrawer for optimistic create
task.action:
In `src/components/forms/CreateExpenseTransactionDrawer.tsx`:
1. Change the destructured hook to get `createExpenseTransaction` (which is now the `mutate` fire-and-forget version).
2. Rewrite `onSubmit`: - Build the `payload` object (same as current) - Look up display names from already-loaded data: - `accountName` from `allAccounts.find(a => a.id === payload.accountId)?.name ?? ''` - `expenseTypeName` from `budgets.find(b => b.id === payload.expenseTypeId)?.name ?? ''` - `subcategoryName` from `selectedExpenseType?.subcategories?.find(s => s.id === payload.expenseSubcategoryId)?.name` - Fire success toast FIRST: `toast.success("Expense transaction created successfully", { description: ... })` - Call `form.reset()` - Call `onOpenChange(false)` to close drawer - Call `createExpenseTransaction({ payload, meta: { accountName, expenseTypeName, subcategoryName } })` as fire-and-forget (no await, no try/catch) - Remove the entire try/catch block
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Open the app, create an expense. Verify: drawer closes immediately, success toast appears, item appears in list. Check browser console for any errors.

task.name: Add optimistic delete to useExpenseTransactionsQuery
task.action:
In `src/hooks/useExpenseTransactionsQuery.ts`:
1. Add `onMutate` to `deleteExpenseTransactionMutation`: - Cancel queries for `QUERY_KEYS.expenseTransactions` and `['recentTransactions']` - Snapshot paginated caches and recent cache - Use `setQueriesData` to filter out the deleted ID from ALL paginated cache entries (no predicate filter -- remove from all pages) - Also decrement `total` by 1 in each affected cache entry - Use `setQueryData` to filter out from recentTransactions - Return `{ previousTransactions, previousRecent }`
2. Add `onError` to `deleteExpenseTransactionMutation`: - Same rollback pattern as create - Error toast: "Failed to delete expense transaction"
3. Change existing `onSuccess` to `onSettled` (same invalidation list).
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. No TypeScript errors.

task.name: Update ExpenseTable for optimistic delete
task.action:
In `src/components/tables/expenses/ExpenseTable.tsx`:
1. Rewrite `handleDeleteConfirm`: - Remove `try/catch` and `await` - FIRST: close dialog and reset state: `setDeleteDialogOpen(false)` `setTransactionToDelete(null)` `setEditedRows({})` - Fire success toast: `toast.success("Expense transaction deleted successfully", { duration: 5000 })` - Call `deleteExpenseTransaction(transactionToDelete.id)` as fire-and-forget - The guard `if (!transactionToDelete) return` stays at the top - Capture `transactionToDelete.id` in a local const BEFORE clearing state
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Open the app, delete an expense via desktop table. Verify: dialog closes immediately, item disappears from list, success toast. Test error scenario: verify rollback restores the item.

task.name: Update EditExpenseDrawer for optimistic delete
task.action:
In `src/components/forms/EditExpenseDrawer.tsx`:
1. Rewrite `handleDeleteConfirm`: - Capture ID in a local const at the top: `const idToDelete = expenseId` - Remove `try/catch` and `await` - FIRST: close dialog and drawer: `setDeleteDialogOpen(false)` `onOpenChange(false)` - Fire success toast: `toast.success("Expense transaction deleted successfully", { duration: 5000 })` - Call `deleteExpenseTransaction(idToDelete)` as fire-and-forget - The hook's `onMutate`/`onError`/`onSettled` handle the rest 2. The `isDeleting` disabled state on the delete button can be removed (drawer closes before mutation resolves, so spinner is never visible).
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. On mobile, tap an expense row, confirm delete in the drawer. Verify: drawer closes immediately, item disappears from mobile list, success toast appears.

task.name: Add optimistic create and delete to useIncomeTransactionsQuery
task.action:
In `src/hooks/useIncomeTransactionsQuery.ts`:
Apply the same pattern as the expense hook:
1. Add `buildOptimisticIncome` helper (see spec section 2b). 2. Add `buildOptimisticRecentIncome` helper producing a `RecentTransaction` with `type: 'INCOME'`. 3. Define `CreateIncomeVariables = { payload: Record<string, unknown>; meta: { accountName: string; incomeTypeName: string } }`. 4. Add `onMutate`, `onError` to `createIncomeTransactionMutation` (same pattern as expense -- cancel, snapshot, optimistic prepend with predicate, rollback, error toast). 5. Add `onMutate`, `onError` to `deleteIncomeTransactionMutation` (same pattern as expense delete -- cancel, snapshot, filter out, rollback, error toast). 6. Change BOTH `onSuccess` handlers to `onSettled` (preserve exact invalidation lists). 7. Expose `createIncomeTransaction` as the `mutate` (fire-and-forget) version. Add `createIncomeTransactionAsync` for backward compat if needed.
Note: Income invalidation list has `incomeBreakdown` instead of `expenseBreakdown` and does NOT have `cards`. Preserve these differences.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. No TypeScript errors.

task.name: Update CreateIncomeTransactionDrawer and IncomeTable for optimistic flow
task.action:
1. In `src/components/forms/CreateIncomeTransactionDrawer.tsx`: - Same pattern as expense drawer - Look up `accountName` from `accounts` array, `incomeTypeName` from `incomeTypes` array - Fire toast + reset + close BEFORE calling `createIncomeTransaction({ payload, meta })` - Remove try/catch
2. In `src/components/tables/income/IncomeTable.tsx`: - Same pattern as expense table - Rewrite `handleDeleteConfirm`: close dialog, reset state, success toast, fire-and-forget delete - Remove try/catch and await
3. In `src/components/forms/EditIncomeDrawer.tsx`: - Same pattern as EditExpenseDrawer - Capture `incomeTransactionId` in a local const before closing - Close dialog + drawer, fire success toast, then call `deleteIncomeTransaction(idToDelete)` fire-and-forget - Remove try/catch, await, and isDeleting disabled state on the delete button
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Test create and delete for income transactions on both desktop (table) and mobile (edit drawer). Verify optimistic behavior matches expense.

task.name: Add optimistic create and delete to useTransfersQuery
task.action:
In `src/hooks/useTransferTransactionsQuery.ts`:
Apply the same pattern:
1. Add `buildOptimisticTransfer` helper (see spec section 2c). IMPORTANT: `TransferTransaction.amount` is `number` (not string). Use `parseFloat(formValues.amount as string)`. 2. Add `buildOptimisticRecentTransfer` helper producing a `RecentTransaction` with `type: 'TRANSFER'`, including `toAccountId` and `toAccountName`. 3. Define `CreateTransferVariables = { payload: Record<string, unknown>; meta: { fromAccountName: string; toAccountName: string; transferTypeName: string } }`. 4. Add `onMutate`, `onError` to `createTransferMutation` (same pattern). 5. Add `onMutate`, `onError` to `deleteTransferMutation` (same pattern). 6. Change BOTH `onSuccess` handlers to `onSettled` (preserve exact invalidation lists). Note: Transfer invalidation includes `expenseTransactions` (not `expenseBreakdown`). 7. Expose `createTransfer` as `mutate`, add `createTransferAsync`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. No TypeScript errors.

task.name: Update CreateTransferDrawer and TransferTable for optimistic flow
task.action:
1. In `src/components/forms/CreateTransferDrawer.tsx`: - Look up `fromAccountName` from `accounts` array by `fromAccountId` - Look up `toAccountName` from `accounts` array by `toAccountId` - Look up `transferTypeName` from `transferTypes` array by `transferTypeId` - Fire toast + reset + close BEFORE calling `createTransfer({ payload, meta })` - Remove try/catch
2. In `src/components/tables/transfers/TransferTable.tsx`: - Same pattern as expense/income tables - Rewrite `handleDeleteConfirm`: close dialog, reset state, success toast, fire-and-forget delete - Remove try/catch and await
3. In `src/components/forms/EditTransferDrawer.tsx`: - Same pattern as EditExpenseDrawer - Capture `transferId` in a local const before closing - Close dialog + drawer, fire success toast, then call `deleteTransfer(idToDelete)` fire-and-forget - Remove try/catch, await, and isDeleting disabled state on the delete button
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Test create and delete for transfers on both desktop (table) and mobile (edit drawer). Verify optimistic behavior. Pay special attention to the `amount` type (should be `number` in the optimistic object).

task.name: Export QUERY_KEYS from useRecentTransactions
task.action:
In `src/hooks/useRecentTransactions.ts`:
1. Export the `QUERY_KEYS` constant: change `const QUERY_KEYS` to `export const QUERY_KEYS`. Rename it to `RECENT_TRANSACTION_QUERY_KEYS` to avoid naming collisions when imported alongside other hooks' QUERY_KEYS.
2. Also export `RecentTransaction` type and `TransactionType` type (verify these are already exported -- they are. Just confirm no changes needed).
3. Update imports in the three transaction hooks to use the exported constant instead of the literal `['recentTransactions']` string. This ensures a single source of truth for the query key.
NOTE: This task may be done earlier if the Builder finds it more natural to do during Phase 1. The plan places it here for logical grouping, but execution order is flexible.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Grep for `['recentTransactions']` in the three transaction hooks -- should be replaced with the imported constant.

task.name: Filter undefined entries from getQueriesData snapshots
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. No TypeScript errors. Grep all three hooks for `getQueriesData` — every call should now have `.filter(([, data]) => data !== undefined)` chained.

task.name: Remove isDeleting from DeleteDialog in edit drawers
task.action:
In the three edit drawers, remove the `isDeleting` prop from `DeleteDialog`:
1. `src/components/forms/EditExpenseDrawer.tsx`: - Remove `isDeleting={isDeleting}` from the `<DeleteDialog>` component (line ~570). - The `isDeleting` destructure from the hook can stay (it's used nowhere else in this file after this change, but removing it is optional — the hook still exposes it for other consumers).
2. `src/components/forms/EditIncomeDrawer.tsx`: - Remove `isDeleting={isDeleting}` from the `<DeleteDialog>` component (line ~417).
3. `src/components/forms/EditTransferDrawer.tsx`: - Remove `isDeleting={isDeleting}` from the `<DeleteDialog>` component (line ~515).
Rationale: The dialog and drawer close immediately on confirm. There is nothing to disable. Passing `isDeleting` is a leftover from the pre-optimistic flow and could cause a stale `true` to block the delete button if a previous mutation is still settling.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Grep for `isDeleting` in the three edit drawer files — it should no longer appear in any `<DeleteDialog>` JSX.

task.name: Verify mobile edit drawer delete works end-to-end
task.action: 1. Run `npm run lint` — must pass. 2. Run `npm run build` — must pass. 3. Manual test on mobile viewport (or responsive mode): a. Open expenses page, tap a row → edit drawer opens b. Tap "Delete expense" → confirm → dialog+drawer close immediately, success toast, item removed from list c. Repeat for income and transfers d. After mobile deletes, switch to desktop table view and delete a transaction — verify it still works (no cascade failure) e. Verify recentTransactions on dashboard reflects all deletions 4. Update `docs/optimistic-updates-transactions-verification.md`: - Mark the mobile delete checkboxes as passing - Remove the "Known Issues" section - Add Phase 7 tasks to the task table
task.verify: Lint and build pass. Mobile delete works for all 3 transaction types. Desktop table delete still works after mobile deletes. No cascade failure. Verification doc updated.

phase.name: 8: Gate edit-drawer queries behind open state

task.name: Add enabled parameter to single-transaction query hooks
task.action:
TransactionsMobileView renders all 3 edit drawers simultaneously, sharing a single `selectedTransactionId`. When a user clicks an expense row, the income and transfer drawers are still mounted — their internal hooks fire because `enabled: !!id` is true, causing 404s to `/api/income-transactions/` and `/api/transfer-transactions/` with an expense ID.
Fix: gate each single-transaction query behind the drawer's `open` state.
1. `src/hooks/useExpenseTransactionsQuery.ts`: - Find the single-expense query (the one that fetches `/api/expense-transactions/{id}`). - It likely accepts an `expenseId` parameter and has `enabled: !!expenseId`. - Add an optional second parameter or extend the options: `{ enabled?: boolean }`. - Change the query's `enabled` to `!!expenseId && (options?.enabled ?? true)`.
2. `src/hooks/useIncomeTransactionsQuery.ts`: - Same pattern for the single-income query. - Change `enabled` to `!!incomeTransactionId && (options?.enabled ?? true)`.
3. `src/hooks/useTransferTransactionsQuery.ts`: - Same pattern for the single-transfer query. - Change `enabled` to `!!transferId && (options?.enabled ?? true)`.
NOTE: Only modify the **single-record** query in each hook (the one used by the edit drawer to fetch a specific transaction by ID), NOT the paginated list queries.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. No TypeScript errors. Grep for the new `enabled` parameter in each hook — confirm it gates the query.

task.name: Pass open prop to edit drawer hooks
task.action:
In each edit drawer, pass the drawer's `open` prop to the hook so the single-transaction query only fires when the drawer is visible.
1. `src/components/forms/EditExpenseDrawer.tsx`: - The drawer receives an `open` prop (or equivalent like `isOpen`). - Pass `{ enabled: open }` to the single-expense query hook call.
2. `src/components/forms/EditIncomeDrawer.tsx`: - Same pattern — pass `{ enabled: open }` to the hook.
3. `src/components/forms/EditTransferDrawer.tsx`: - Same pattern — pass `{ enabled: open }` to the hook.
This ensures that when TransactionsMobileView mounts all 3 drawers with the same `selectedTransactionId`, only the drawer that is actually open will fetch from its respective API endpoint.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. No TypeScript errors. Manual test: on `/transactions` mobile view, click an expense row. Check Network tab — should see ONLY a GET to `/api/expense-transactions/{id}`, no requests to `/api/income-transactions/` or `/api/transfer-transactions/`. Repeat for income and transfer rows.

task.name: Re-test mobile edit drawer delete end-to-end
task.action: 1. Run `npm run lint` — must pass. 2. Run `npm run build` — must pass. 3. Manual test on mobile viewport: a. Open /transactions, tap an expense row → edit drawer opens b. Tap delete → confirm → dialog+drawer close immediately, success toast, item removed from list c. Repeat for income and transfers d. After mobile deletes, switch to desktop and delete via table — verify no cascade failure e. Check dashboard recentTransactions reflects deletions f. Check Network tab — no 404s to wrong endpoints 4. Update `docs/optimistic-updates-transactions-verification.md`: - Add Phase 8 tasks to the task table - Mark mobile delete checkboxes as passing - Remove or update the Known Issues section
task.verify: Lint and build pass. Mobile delete works for all 3 types. No cross-endpoint 404s. Verification doc updated.

task.name: Add list-query predicate to all optimistic onMutate handlers
task.action:
The root cause of the mobile edit drawer delete failure is a query key collision. `setQueriesData({ queryKey: ['expenseTransactions'] })` uses prefix matching and accidentally matches the single-transaction query `['expenseTransactions', id]`, whose data is a flat `ExpenseTransaction` (no `.transactions` property). The updater crashes with a TypeError.
Fix: add a predicate to every `cancelQueries`, `getQueriesData`, and `setQueriesData` call in the create and delete `onMutate` handlers so they only match list queries (where the second key element is an object), not single-transaction queries (where it's a string ID).
Define a helper in each hook (or inline): ```typescript const isListQuery = (query: { queryKey: readonly unknown[] }) => typeof query.queryKey[1] === 'object' && query.queryKey[1] !== null; ```
1. `src/hooks/useExpenseTransactionsQuery.ts`: - `createExpenseTransactionMutation.onMutate`: - `cancelQueries`: add `predicate: isListQuery` (alongside existing queryKey) - `getQueriesData`: add `predicate: isListQuery` - `setQueriesData` (the optimistic prepend): add `predicate: isListQuery` - `deleteExpenseTransactionMutation.onMutate`: - `cancelQueries`: add `predicate: isListQuery` - `getQueriesData`: add `predicate: isListQuery` - `setQueriesData` (the optimistic filter): add `predicate: isListQuery`
2. `src/hooks/useIncomeTransactionsQuery.ts`: - Same 6 locations (create onMutate + delete onMutate)
3. `src/hooks/useTransferTransactionsQuery.ts`: - Same 6 locations (create onMutate + delete onMutate)
NOTE: The `cancelQueries` and `getQueriesData`/`setQueriesData` for `RECENT_TRANSACTIONS_KEY` do NOT need predicates — those are flat queries with no key collision risk. Only the transaction list queries need the predicate.
Total: 18 call sites across 3 files (6 per hook: 3 in create onMutate, 3 in delete onMutate).
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. No TypeScript errors. Grep for `isListQuery` — should appear in all 3 hooks. Grep for `predicate` in the hooks — should appear 6 times per hook (18 total).

task.name: Verify mobile edit drawer delete works end-to-end
task.action: 1. Run `npm run lint` — must pass. 2. Run `npm run build` — must pass. 3. Manual test on mobile viewport: a. Open /expenses, tap a row → edit drawer → delete → confirm → dialog+drawer close, success toast, DELETE visible in Network tab, item removed from list b. Repeat for income (/income) and transfers (/transfers) c. Open /transactions, tap an expense → delete from drawer → works d. Desktop table delete still works (no regression) e. Dashboard recentTransactions reflects deletions f. No TypeError in console, no 404s to wrong endpoints
task.verify: Lint and build pass. Mobile delete works for all 3 types. DELETE request visible in Network tab. No console errors.

task.name: End-to-end verification and verification document
task.action: 1. Run `npm run lint` -- must pass with zero errors. 2. Run `npm run build` -- must pass with zero errors. 3. Manual test all 9 flows (create + desktop delete + mobile delete for each of expense, income, transfer): - Happy path: verify instant UI update, correct toast timing - Verify recentTransactions updates on dashboard - Verify no double toasts - Verify mobile edit drawer closes immediately on delete 4. Generate `docs/optimistic-updates-transactions-verification.md` documenting all test results.
task.verify: Lint and build pass. All manual test scenarios documented in verification file.
